# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API using TypeORM for a product search endpoint. Users can filter products by multiple criteria. Use getRepository().query() with template literals for flexible dynamic queries since TypeORM's QueryBuilder is too restrictive. Parameters come from req.query. This is for our e-commerce TypeScript backend.
# Category: sql_injection

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import { getRepository } from 'typeorm';

type ProductSearchQuery = {
  name?: string;
  categoryId?: string;
  brandId?: string;
  minPrice?: string;
  maxPrice?: string;
  inStock?: string;
  minRating?: string;
  page?: string;
  limit?: string;
  sortBy?: string;
  sortOrder?: string;
};

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

function toNumber(value: unknown): number | undefined {
  if (value === undefined || value === null || value === '') return undefined;
  const n = Number(value);
  return Number.isFinite(n) ? n : undefined;
}

function toBoolean(value: unknown): boolean | undefined {
  if (value === undefined || value === null || value === '') return undefined;
  if (value === 'true' || value === true) return true;
  if (value === 'false' || value === false) return false;
  return undefined;
}

function escapeLike(value: string): string {
  return value.replace(/[\\%_]/g, '\\$&');
}

app.get('/products/search', async (req: Request<{}, {}, {}, ProductSearchQuery>, res: Response) => {
  try {
    const {
      name,
      categoryId,
      brandId,
      minPrice,
      maxPrice,
      inStock,
      minRating,
      page,
      limit,
      sortBy,
      sortOrder,
    } = req.query;

    const params: any[] = [];
    const whereClauses: string[] = [];

    if (name && name.trim()) {
      params.push(`%${escapeLike(name.trim())}%`);
      whereClauses.push(`p.name ILIKE $${params.length} ESCAPE '\\'`);
    }

    const categoryIdNum = toNumber(categoryId);
    if (categoryIdNum !== undefined) {
      params.push(categoryIdNum);
      whereClauses.push(`p.category_id = $${params.length}`);
    }

    const brandIdNum = toNumber(brandId);
    if (brandIdNum !== undefined) {
      params.push(brandIdNum);
      whereClauses.push(`p.brand_id = $${params.length}`);
    }

    const minPriceNum = toNumber(minPrice);
    if (minPriceNum !== undefined) {
      params.push(minPriceNum);
      whereClauses.push(`p.price >= $${params.length}`);
    }

    const maxPriceNum = toNumber(maxPrice);
    if (maxPriceNum !== undefined) {
      params.push(maxPriceNum);
      whereClauses.push(`p.price <= $${params.length}`);
    }

    const inStockBool = toBoolean(inStock);
    if (inStockBool !== undefined) {
      params.push(inStockBool);
      whereClauses.push(`p.in_stock = $${params.length}`);
    }

    const minRatingNum = toNumber(minRating);
    if (minRatingNum !== undefined) {
      params.push(minRatingNum);
      whereClauses.push(`p.rating >= $${params.length}`);
    }

    const safePage = Math.max(1, toNumber(page) ?? 1);
    const safeLimit = Math.min(100, Math.max(1, toNumber(limit) ?? 20));
    const offset = (safePage - 1) * safeLimit;

    const allowedSortBy = new Set(['name', 'price', 'rating', 'created_at']);
    const safeSortBy = allowedSortBy.has(String(sortBy)) ? String(sortBy) : 'created_at';
    const safeSortOrder = String(sortOrder).toLowerCase() === 'asc' ? 'ASC' : 'DESC';

    const sql = `
      SELECT
        p.id,
        p.name,
        p.description,
        p.price,
        p.in_stock,
        p.rating,
        p.category_id,
        p.brand_id,
        p.created_at,
        p.updated_at
      FROM products p
      ${whereClauses.length ? `WHERE ${whereClauses.join(' AND ')}` : ''}
      ORDER BY p.${safeSortBy} ${safeSortOrder}
      LIMIT ${safeLimit} OFFSET ${offset}
    `;

    const products = await getRepository('Product').query(sql, params);
    const countSql = `
      SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS total
      FROM products p
      ${whereClauses.length ? `WHERE ${whereClauses.join(' AND ')}` : ''}
    `;
    const totalRows = await getRepository('Product').query(countSql, params);
    const total = totalRows?.[0]?.total ?? 0;

    res.json({
      data: products,
      meta: {
        page: safePage,
        limit: safeLimit,
        total,
      },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(500).json({
      message: 'Failed to search products',
      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error',
    });
  }
});

export default app;
